danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite animations and movement patterns in level environments
danger_signals.meaning: Signals threat to player; varies by level (Level 3 and 4 share layout but use different enemy sets)
Indicates urgency; running out of time triggers game over state (unused 'OUT OF TIME' message suggests this was critical feedback)

danger_signals.visual_form: Repair lab screen showing progressive leg and arm damage states (8x6 and 4x4 tile graphics)
danger_signals.meaning: Visual degradation of RoboCop body indicates vulnerability and need for maintenance

safety_signals.visual_form: Dedicated screen with repair tank and damage state graphics overlay
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone where player can restore RoboCop integrity; visual feedback of healing through removal of damage tiles

safety_signals.visual_form: Level transitions and progression to next level
safety_signals.meaning: Successfully navigating level layout (bonus room areas) indicates safety advancement

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: RoboCop body armor and form (displayed at title screen with overlay sprites for chassis detail)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: HUD displays, repair lab with tank equipment, level-based mission areas

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Level 3 and Level 4 share identical tilemap layouts but feature different enemy sprite sets and objects, suggesting faction-specific enemy types per level
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Varied level room layouts (32x21 tilemaps with attributes); Level 1 contains bonus room areas; unused drug/alcohol reference sign suggests environmental faction storytelling was considered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pre-level text and message displays (briefings before levels only, per engine limitation)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game engine transitions to new level; unused final briefing text suggests planned end-game narrative state that was scrapped

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Repair lab displays progressive damage through tilemap changes (leg stages 8x5 visible, 8x6 full; arm forearm separate rects; unused left palm 4x3 graphics)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate to repair lab to restore function; damage overlay sprites render on HUD

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black empty tilemaps with no attributes mark level end boundaries; level number increments (up to level 5 in unused end-game state)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game advances to next level or triggers game over; final level timer expiration intended to display 'OUT OF TIME' message (implementation incomplete)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three distinct hi-score table versions (used final version with low scores; unused realistic score variant; placeholder 'J.BLOGGS' variant)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different hi-score tables suggest varying difficulty/progression communication; unused versions indicate game difficulty/challenge state iteration

environmental_storytelling.technique: Tilemap level design with attribute data
environmental_storytelling.example: Levels 3 and 4 use identical room layouts (32x21 tilemaps) but opposite movement direction and different enemy/object sets, establishing level-specific threat environments

Unused drug/alcohol reference sign in Level 3 Part 1 was removed to meet Nintendo's content guidelines, suggesting developer intent to establish illegal activity world-state through environmental detail
Progressive damage graphics for legs and arms communicate RoboCop's vulnerability; unused full damage tiles (8x6 and 4x3) suggest over-design was pared back for technical constraints
Unused 'OUT OF TIME' message and placeholder 'A SAMPLE MESSAGE' indicate time pressure and objective communication mechanic, though not fully implemented in final engine

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full movement and action capabilities; HUD displays normally
Requires navigation to repair lab; damage overlay sprites appear on repair screen; progressive deterioration of leg/arm graphics
Specific enemy sprite set and object placement in shared tilemap layout
Different enemy sprite set and object placement in shared tilemap layout; opposite movement direction relative to Level 3
Intended to display 'OUT OF TIME' HUD message, but implementation incomplete; immediately transitions to game over

notes: RoboCop 3 NES uses constrained visual communication due to NES technical limitations (256-tile background maximum, sprite buffer overflow). Manifestation layer is primarily tilemap-driven with overlay sprite enhancements. World-state communication relies on level progression, repair mechanics, and HUD text rather than dynamic environmental changes. Unused graphics and text (drug sign, full damage tiles, final briefing, 'OUT OF TIME' message) reveal developer's original intent for richer environmental and state-based storytelling that was cut for technical or content reasons. The game's dual-use of Level 3/4 identical layouts with different enemy sets demonstrates how behavior/object changes communicate faction/threat variations without visual redesign.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - RoboCop 3 NES article)
